rulings.Case Title: FAWZI ZEIDAN VS. BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 18, 2011, line 4, DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant¿s Motion for Summary Judgment is denied. Plaintiff raises triable issues of fact as to each of the claims, including whether or not his 1st ands 2nd causes of action are time barred. Unclean Hands and Laches are equitable doctrines and Plaintiff is not seeking equitable relief. Therefore, they are inapplicable to this case. There are triable issues of fact regarding the Estoppel Certificate and the fact that it fails to mention the Addendum Option to Renew. The Defendant has failed to show evidence that Plaintiff does not possess or cannot reasonably obtain evidence to support the negligence or tort of another claims. As to the Breach of Contract claim, defendant has shifted the burden by presenting evidence that there was no enforceable contract. However, Plaintiff raises a triable issue of fact by presenting conflicting evidence. =(302/LMG)
